title: Real-estate alt-financing platform — product-grilling Qs, Sept 2024
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir's product-grilling questions about a real-estate alt-financing platform he was being pitched on or considering building (Sep 28 2024, written from Shanghai).
  • Question stack:
    • Is it connecting buyers to alt-financing forms or actually doing the financing itself?
    • Target niche: wholesalers?
    • Is it like a job-listing model where seller posts, buyer applies, seller picks?
    • What kind of listing information — Zillow plus a financing layer?
    • Would this remove the need for scavenging deals (just filter searches)?
    • Best rollout geography to "run deep first."
  • References two real platforms as comparables: Subject2 (creative real-estate financing) and InvestorLift (off-market deal marketplace).

Key open question

"Is the platform simply connecting buyers to alt financing forms or actually doing the financing?"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Confirms Yahir was shopping the real-estate flip space as a possible vertical even mid-China — adjacent to the 2020 09 23 how does he scale quick flips note from 2020 (real-estate thinking was years long).
  • This pattern of product-grilling-as-due-diligence is the same template he later refines into the 2026 03 13 how are they approaching true ai result accuracy competitor-grilling questions and the piercing prompts system.
  • Subject2 + InvestorLift naming reveals a specific industry-research depth in real estate that doesn't appear elsewhere — worth tagging as a real estate flips curiosity thread.

Open questions

  • Who pitched him this? A friend, a Twitter person, or a self-generated idea?
  • Did this lead anywhere, or did the Brotein / Rankett focus crowd it out?
